qa_pairs.question: In the late '60s and early '70s, how did some people obtain free long-distance telephone calls for years after buying a single box of Cap'n Crunch cereal?
qa_pairs.answer: By using the toy whistle from Cap'n Crunch cereal, which happened to produce a 2,600 Hz tone that could trick phone systems into giving free long-distance calls.

qa_pairs.question: UK government ministers transport important paperwork in 'red boxes' that resemble a briefcase. Why are some lined with lead, and what other design feature means you can't forget to lock them?
qa_pairs.answer: The lead lining made them heavy enough to sink if lost overboard, and the handle is on the same side as the hinge so if they aren't locked, the papers fall out when you pick them up.

qa_pairs.question: In the early 1980s, millions of people in the United States gladly purchased something for twice the advertised price. What was the problem and the common workaround?
qa_pairs.answer: Gasoline had gone over $1 per gallon, but many pumps couldn't display prices above 99.9 cents, so stations sold it by the half-gallon and customers doubled the displayed price.

qa_pairs.question: As a sneaky tactic, sometimes an enemy ship would pretend to belong to a different country before launching a surprise attack. This is the origin of which 18th century phrase?
A man goes to the headquarters of a large company to meet two people. He takes out a chainsaw and then puts it away again a few minutes later, unused. The CEO gladly pays the man a large fee. Why?
qa_pairs.answer: He was an IT consultant testing whether the company's backup servers really worked; threatening to cut into a server with a chainsaw made the head of IT reveal that he wasn't confident in the backup system.

qa_pairs.question: In 2013, Penguin Classics re-released a series of five books by an English author. One novel had the title and author's name indented on the front cover, and then covered in rectangles of thin black foil. Why?
qa_pairs.answer: It was George Orwell's Nineteen Eighty-Four, and the black foil was meant to resemble censorship/redaction; because the title and author were indented, the foil would wear away over time and reveal them.

qa_pairs.question: Why is 2nd Street the most common street name in the US?
qa_pairs.answer: Because many towns have numbered streets, but some 1st Streets are instead called Main Street, so 2nd Street appears more often overall.
